questions.question: What is the primary danger of focusing on 'Impressions' as your main LinkedIn metric?

options.text: LinkedIn will charge you more for high impressions.
It is a vanity metric that doesn't tell you if the 'Right' people are seeing your content or if they are taking action.
High impressions cause the algorithm to hide your future posts.
It makes your profile look unprofessional.

questions.explanation: Reach without relevance is noise. For a solo founder, a small number of views from high-intent ICPs is infinitely more valuable than a million views from an unrelated audience.

questions.question: What does the 'Drift Score' (Profile Views per Post) measure?

options.text: How fast you are growing your follower count.
How effective your content/comments are at intriguing a user enough to click your name and visit your 'Landing Page' (Profile).
The number of likes you get on average.
The technical speed of your LinkedIn app.

questions.explanation: The profile visit is the transition point between 'Seeing a Post' and 'Considering a Relationship.' A high drift score means your authority/insight signals are working.

questions.question: If your 'Top Job Titles' demographics show 'Recruiters' and 'Students' instead of your ICP, what is the most likely 'Fix'?

options.text: Delete those users from your network.
Use more niche-specific practitioner keywords and remove broad/generic hashtags.
Stop posting content altogether.
Pay for LinkedIn Premium.

questions.explanation: Generic content attracts a generic audience. To reach decision-makers, you must speak the specific language of their pain and industry.

questions.question: What is the probable cause of 'High Engagement but No Profile Views'?

options.text: Your posts are too long.
Your content is 'Entertaining' or 'Relatable' but lacks enough 'Authority' (A) or 'Proof' (P) to make the reader curious about your expertise.
Your profile is set to private.
You are posting at the wrong time of day.

questions.explanation: People like relatable posts, but they visit the profiles of experts. Ensure you are balancing relatability with deep practitioner insight.

questions.question: Why is 'Dwell Time' (the amount of time a user spends looking at a post) important for your algorithm reach?

options.text: It isn't; only likes matter.
LinkedIn's algorithm treats long dwell time as a signal of high-quality content and shows it to more people.
It automatically converts the user into a lead.
It helps you win the 'Creator of the Year' award.

questions.explanation: This is why carousels and well-structured long-form text are so powerful—they force the user to stop and engage with your authority for longer.

questions.question: Assume you have a post with 1,000 views. 50% are 'Founders' (Your ICP) and 50% are 'Entry Level Developers'. How would you adjust your 'Hook' or 'Authority' signal in your next post to increase the % of Founders while maintaining or increasing the total views? What specific 'Founder Pain' would you lean into?
promptHints: Think about language that an entry-level person wouldn't understand but a founder would.
Consider using a hook that specifically addresses a founder's unique pressure (e.g., 'The cost of a bad hire...').
